title: OAP Commands
description: Schema for the command ingestion and discovery capability (io.oap.agents.commands).

command.description: A command sent to an OAP-compliant service. Uses the CloudEvent 1.0 specification as wire format. The data property is validated against the JSON Schema at the dataschema URI, which is hosted by the ingestion API.

source.description: URI identifying the sender (e.g. a Process Manager, a UI, another service).

type.description: Command type identifier (e.g. ProposeCounter, SubmitOrder). Must match a type in the command catalogue.

dataschema.description: URI to the JSON Schema for the data property. Hosted by the ingestion API. The API dereferences this URI and validates data against it before queuing.

time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the command was created.

data.description: The command payload. Structure is domain-specific and validated against the schema at dataschema.

commandCatalogueEntry.description: An entry in the command catalogue — one available command type with its schema URI.

dataschema.description: URI to the JSON Schema for this command's data payload.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this command does.

commandCatalogue.description: Response body for GET /commands. Lists all command types this service accepts, with their schema URIs. This is the discovery surface for callers (Process Managers, AI agents, UIs) to learn what they can send.
